firm.name: Taurus Advisory
firm.oneLiner: Taurus Advisory is an M&A boutique for the upper mid-market, focused on company sales, acquisitions, and succession planning.

evidence: "Während der Due Diligence werden wir für Sie ein professionelles Datenraummanagement betreiben."
"die Befüllung des Datenraums mit sämtlichen Dokumenten und die Koordination sämtlicher Besucher des Datenraums"

fitNotes: Clear fit for an AI-native deal platform because they run structured sell-side and buy-side processes and explicitly manage buyer outreach and deal documents.
They mention data room management, which suggests a workflow with recurring document collection, Q&A, and diligence coordination.
Their sector coverage is broad across mid-market industries, so a platform could help standardize repeatable process steps across mandates.
They emphasize personal, discreet, high-touch advisory, so any AI tooling would need to support, not replace, the advisor-led process.

Taurus Advisory: Klaus Schmitte im Podcast 'Leading with a Laughter'

play.angle: Lead with the fact that Taurus Advisory already runs a professional data room process during diligence, because that is the clearest sign they feel the pain Fintalo solves. Position Fintalo as a way to replace email, Excel, and scattered document coordination with a single controlled workflow for NDA handling, buyer management, staged document release, and deal tracking. Avoid sounding like a generic CRM or a heavy enterprise system, and avoid overemphasizing automation at the expense of the partner-led, high-trust advisory style they clearly value.

objections.objection: We already manage diligence and buyer communication ourselves.
objections.counter: That is exactly the workflow Fintalo is meant to support, not replace. It keeps the partner in control while reducing the manual email, document chasing, and version drift that slow deals down.

objections.objection: Our process is bespoke, a platform may be too rigid.
objections.counter: Fintalo is designed to mirror how advisors actually run mandates, with stage-gated release and flexible buyer management rather than a fixed one-size-fits-all process.

objections.objection: We do not want to add another tool for the team to learn.
objections.counter: Fair concern. The goal would be to show whether Fintalo can consolidate work you already do across email, Excel, and the data room into one place, so the team uses fewer tools overall.

openingEmail.subject: Quick idea for your diligence process
openingEmail.body:
Hi Taurus Advisory team,

I noticed you mention that during due diligence you run a professional data room management process. Fintalo is an AI-native M&A operating system that helps advisors keep NDA workflows, buyer coordination, staged document release, and deal tracking in one place instead of across email and Excel.

Given the volume of mandates you handle, I thought it might be worth a short look to see whether it could reduce the manual coordination around each process without changing your advisory style. Would you be open to a brief demo?

Best,
openingEmail.groundedFact: I noticed you mention that during due diligence you run a professional data room management process.

unverified: team_size_estimate, exact headcount not explicitly stated, counted 19 named team members on the about page.
cities, only Bad Soden and Wien are explicitly stated, office street addresses not provided.
tombstone_count, 250 is stated on the home page, but the site also shows many individual deal tombstones without a complete count.
has_client_portal, no client login or portal mentioned.
people.focus, no explicit functional focus stated for the named leaders.
signals, some news items are shown without dates in the page list, so only dated items were used.
process_email_based: the site describes hands-on advisory but never names email, Excel or spreadsheets as the deal-management tool; not counted as an email-based process.
